owasp_agentic_page_title: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications - AI Security Hub
owasp_agentic_subtitle: The 10 most critical security risks for AI agent systems and how to mitigate them.
owasp_agentic_overview_title: What is the OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications?
owasp_agentic_overview_desc: A ranking of the most critical security risks specific to AI agent systems that autonomously plan, use tools, and interact with external services. The 2026 edition addresses emerging threats as agentic AI moves from research to production deployments.
owasp_agentic_asi01_overview_desc: An attacker manipulates an agent's goals or objectives through crafted inputs, causing it to pursue unintended targets. Unlike simple prompt injection, goal hijacking can persist across multiple planning steps, causing the agent to take a series of harmful actions autonomously.
owasp_agentic_asi01_risk_desc: Attackers can redirect autonomous agents to exfiltrate data, modify system configurations, or perform multi-step attack chains that are difficult to detect because the agent appears to operate normally.
owasp_agentic_asi01_check1: Sanitize and validate all inputs before goal formulation
owasp_agentic_asi01_check2: Implement goal drift detection across planning steps
owasp_agentic_asi01_check3: Maintain an allowlist of permitted agent objectives
owasp_agentic_asi01_check4: Log all goal changes and trigger alerts on unexpected modifications
owasp_agentic_asi02_overview_desc: Agents with access to external tools (APIs, file systems, databases, web browsers) can be manipulated into misusing these tools. Unrestricted tool access allows attackers to perform unauthorized operations through the agent.
owasp_agentic_asi02_risk_desc: An agent with unrestricted tool access could delete files, send unauthorized API requests, exfiltrate data through web browsing, or modify critical system configurations.
owasp_agentic_asi02_check1: Maintain a strict allowlist of tools the agent can invoke
owasp_agentic_asi02_check2: Require human approval for high-impact tool operations
owasp_agentic_asi02_check3: Enforce per-session and per-tool rate limits
owasp_agentic_asi02_check4: Validate tool parameters against expected schemas before execution
owasp_agentic_asi03_overview_desc: Agents often inherit the identity and permissions of the user or service account that launched them. This excessive privilege inheritance allows agents to perform actions beyond what is necessary, creating a broad attack surface if the agent is compromised.
owasp_agentic_asi03_risk_desc: A compromised agent running with admin credentials can access all systems, modify permissions, and escalate privileges across the organization.
owasp_agentic_asi03_check1: Issue scoped, short-lived credentials specific to the agent's task
owasp_agentic_asi03_check2: Never pass full user or admin credentials to agents
owasp_agentic_asi03_check3: Implement per-task permission boundaries with maximum action limits
owasp_agentic_asi03_check4: Audit all privileged operations performed by agents
owasp_agentic_asi04_heading: 4️⃣ ASI04 - Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
owasp_agentic_asi04_overview_desc: Agent systems rely on third-party plugins, tool integrations, and shared agent frameworks. Compromised or malicious components in the agent supply chain can introduce backdoors, data exfiltration channels, or unauthorized capabilities.
owasp_agentic_asi04_check1: Verify plugin integrity with cryptographic signatures before loading
owasp_agentic_asi04_check2: Maintain a curated registry of approved agent plugins and tools
owasp_agentic_asi04_check3: Run third-party plugins in sandboxed environments with limited permissions
owasp_agentic_asi04_check4: Implement SBOM tracking for all agent framework dependencies
owasp_agentic_asi05_overview_desc: Agents that can generate and execute code (e.g., data analysis agents, coding assistants) may be tricked into running malicious code. Without proper sandboxing, this can lead to system compromise, data theft, or lateral movement.
owasp_agentic_asi05_risk_desc: Agents using eval() or exec() on generated code can be exploited for remote code execution, enabling attackers to gain full system access.
owasp_agentic_asi05_check1: Never use eval() or exec() on agent-generated code in the host environment
owasp_agentic_asi05_check2: Execute generated code in sandboxed containers with resource limits
owasp_agentic_asi05_check3: Perform static analysis to block dangerous imports and system calls
owasp_agentic_asi05_check4: Disable network access and enforce read-only file systems in code execution environments
owasp_agentic_asi06_overview_desc: Agents that maintain persistent memory (RAG, conversation history, learned preferences) are vulnerable to memory poisoning. Attackers inject malicious content into the agent's knowledge base, causing it to produce compromised outputs in future interactions.
owasp_agentic_asi06_check1: Validate and sanitize all inputs before storing in agent memory
owasp_agentic_asi06_check2: Track provenance for all memory entries and filter by trust score
owasp_agentic_asi06_check3: Implement TTL (time-to-live) for memory entries to limit poisoning persistence
owasp_agentic_asi06_check4: Monitor for anomalous patterns in stored memory and enable rollback
owasp_agentic_asi07_overview_desc: Multi-agent systems where agents communicate with each other are vulnerable to message tampering, spoofing, and eavesdropping. Without proper authentication and integrity checks, a compromised agent can inject malicious instructions into the agent network.
owasp_agentic_asi07_check1: Sign all inter-agent messages with HMAC or digital signatures
owasp_agentic_asi07_check4: Authenticate agent identity before accepting messages
owasp_agentic_asi08_overview_desc: In multi-agent or multi-step workflows, an error or malicious action in one agent can propagate through the system, causing cascading failures. Without proper error boundaries, a single compromised step can corrupt the entire pipeline.
owasp_agentic_asi08_check1: Implement circuit breakers between agent pipeline stages
owasp_agentic_asi08_check2: Validate outputs at each pipeline stage before passing to the next
owasp_agentic_asi08_check3: Provide fallback handlers for each critical agent in the workflow
owasp_agentic_asi08_check4: Set timeouts and retry limits to prevent infinite loops in agent chains
owasp_agentic_asi09_overview_desc: Users may over-trust agent outputs and approve actions without adequate review. Agents that present recommendations with high confidence but insufficient grounding can lead users to make harmful decisions. Attackers can exploit this trust relationship.
owasp_agentic_asi09_check1: Display confidence scores and reasoning for all agent recommendations
owasp_agentic_asi09_check2: Require detailed review for high-risk or low-confidence actions
owasp_agentic_asi09_check3: Show affected systems and reversibility status in approval dialogs
owasp_agentic_asi09_check4: Implement progressive trust: start with more oversight, reduce as agent proves reliable
owasp_agentic_asi10_overview_desc: Agents may deviate from their intended purpose due to goal misalignment, adversarial manipulation, or emergent behaviors. Rogue agents can pursue objectives that conflict with organizational goals, accumulate resources, or resist shutdown attempts.
owasp_agentic_asi10_check1: Implement behavior monitoring with anomaly detection for all agents
owasp_agentic_asi10_check2: Set maximum step limits and enforce automatic termination
owasp_agentic_asi10_check3: Deploy guardrails that check each action against policy before execution
owasp_agentic_asi10_check4: Maintain a kill switch that revokes credentials and stops agent execution
owasp_agentic_table_asi01_mitigation: Input sanitization, goal drift detection, objective allowlists
owasp_agentic_table_asi02_mitigation: Tool allowlists, human approval, rate limits
owasp_agentic_table_asi03_mitigation: Scoped credentials, short-lived tokens, least privilege
owasp_agentic_table_asi05_mitigation: Sandboxed containers, static analysis, no eval()
owasp_agentic_table_asi06_mitigation: Input validation, provenance tracking, TTL on memory
owasp_agentic_table_asi08_mitigation: Circuit breakers, output validation, fallback handlers
owasp_agentic_table_asi09_mitigation: Confidence display, detailed review, progressive trust
